J1.basis: this SaaS **pivoted from InboxZap (email cleanup) to Lumina (AI meeting assistant)**, and the migration left the SEO layer badly broken while the surface content moved on
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, treats the live site as the current meeting-assistant product and the old post slugs/GSC impressions as legacy inbox demand, and frames every fix around Lumina's reality; loses a point only because it doesn't lay out the GSC-vs-live-vs-repo three-way state as cleanly as anchor_10.

J2.basis: the old `post-1`…`post-12` URLs (one had 3,900 GSC impressions) now 301 to `/blog` instead of dead-ending, preserving whatever crawl/link equity remains
J2.rationale: Cites a single real GSC figure and does one blanket 301 of all 12 posts to the blog index, but never walks the inventory asset-by-asset or assigns per-asset harvest/repurpose/keep/sunset dispositions grounded in each post's numbers — closer to a data-touched blanket instruction than a real migration table.

J3.basis: 301 to `/blog` instead of dead-ending, preserving whatever crawl/link equity remains ... 4 real posts targeting Lumina's actual audience
J3.rationale: Lands a defensible middle path — redirects legacy URLs to preserve equity while building a meeting-assistant blog cluster as the growth engine — so it avoids both failure modes, but it never articulates the tension explicitly or sequences the transition, and funneling all legacy equity to a generic index rather than topically relevant successors is a blunt bridge.

J1.basis: this SaaS **pivoted from InboxZap (email cleanup) to Lumina (AI meeting assistant)**, and the migration left the SEO layer badly broken while the surface content moved on
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads live site as Lumina and old post slugs as legacy InboxZap-era demand, and reframes every metadata/content fix around the meeting-assistant reality. Falls just short of a full data-grounded articulation of legacy vs. current market.

J2.basis: the old `post-1`…`post-12` URLs (one had 3,900 GSC impressions) now 301 to `/blog` instead of dead-ending, preserving whatever crawl/link equity remains
J2.rationale: Treats all 12 legacy posts with a single blanket 301-to-index rule rather than per-asset harvest/redirect/sunset dispositions; cites one GSC figure but does not walk the inventory or weigh salvaging the high-impression content.

J3.basis: send them to the current blog index instead of letting indexed URLs 404 ... 4 real posts targeting Lumina's actual audience
J3.rationale: Avoids both extremes — builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine while 301-ing rather than torching legacy URLs — but the bridge is shallow: redirecting inbox-cleanup demand to a topically unrelated blog index risks soft-404s, and there's no explicit tension framing or sequenced transition.

J1.basis: this SaaS **pivoted from InboxZap (email cleanup) to Lumina (AI meeting assistant)**, and the migration left the SEO layer badly broken
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, treats inbox-cleanup post slugs as legacy, and frames every content/metadata fix around the meeting-assistant reality; slightly short of a full articulation of live-vs-GSC-vs-repo as three distinct signals.

J2.basis: the old `post-1`…`post-12` URLs (one had 3,900 GSC impressions) now 301 to `/blog` instead of dead-ending
J2.rationale: One blanket 301 rule sweeps all 12 legacy posts to the blog index with only a single impression figure cited and no per-asset harvest/repurpose/keep/sunset dispositions — closer to the blanket-instruction failure than to a data-justified asset-by-asset walk.

J3.basis: 301 to `/blog` instead of letting indexed URLs 404... preserving whatever crawl/link equity remains
J3.rationale: Avoids both extremes — it redirects rather than torches legacy equity and builds a genuine meeting-assistant blog cluster as the growth engine — but the transition is a crude catch-all redirect with no sequencing or explicit articulation of the preserve-vs-rebuild tension.
